description_long: This object is a vintage reel-to-reel tape recorder, featuring a wooden rectangular body with a metallic front panel. The front is dominated by two large red reels, one for feeding and one for taking up magnetic tape, with a central spindle and visible tape threading between them. Below the reels, there are analog VU meters, several rotary knobs, toggle switches, and indicator lights, all set into a brushed metal control panel. The sides of the device are plain wood, each with a metallic handle inset for carrying. The top and back are unadorned, emphasizing the utilitarian, boxy design typical of mid-20th-century audio equipment. The overall appearance suggests a robust, professional-grade audio device used for recording or playback in studios or broadcast environments.
description: A vintage reel-to-reel tape recorder with a wooden body, red reels, and a metal control panel featuring analog meters and knobs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden panel with a metallic handle and the profile of the red reels protruding from the front.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the plain wooden back of the device and the tops of the two red reels.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with another metallic handle and the reels' profile, mirroring view 0.
description_view_3: Front view highlighting the two large red reels, analog meters, control knobs, switches, and indicator lights on the metal panel.
attribute_control_interface: physical buttons, rotary dial, toggle switch
